english: Ssk. word for it, which however seems not to agree with its use.
B. and C. or if not, or else, or also, in double-questions after the termination am of the first question.

1. deaf, also to be deaf; _on-pa-pa ('on pa pa), _on-pa-po ('on pa po), _on-po ('on po) a deaf man, _on-pa-mo ('on pa mo), _on-mo ('on mo) a deaf woman Cs.; _on-loṅ ('on long) deaf and blind. - 2. to give, to bring, chiefly as imp. _on-cig ('on cig) Dzl.
with byed-pa (byed pa), to pay attention, to watch, to spy Sch.
1. also _obs ('obs) ditch, trench, pit Dzl.; me-_ob (me 'ob) fiery pit; also fig.: the fire-pool of passions. - 2. v. yob (yob).
1. tamarisk, Myricaria Med. not unfrequent near the rivers of Tibet. - 2. Sch.: 'a town, settlement' (?).
1. dropsy, viz the species anasarca, nif., = pags-ču (pags chu); dbu-_or (dbu 'or) prob. id. Med. - 2. eddy, whirlpool Sch.
1. to put or lay down Cs. - 2. to feed, e. g. a little child W.
clover, trefoil, viz. snail-clover, medic, (Medicago); _ol-táṅ ('ol thang) a plain covered with such clover; *_ol-kyog* ('ol kyog) W. snail.
in a general way, generally speaking, about, _ol-spyir ('ol spyir) id. Sch.; _ol-spyi tsám-du dus mnyám-mo ('ol spyi tsam du dus mnyam mo) they are about contemporaries Tar.
Ld. *oṅ-mol* (ong mol) besom, broom, brush, stag-_ol (stag 'ol) birch-broom, zed-_ol (zed 'ol) hair-broom Cs.
Wdn. an officinal plant; Cs.: '_ol-ma-sa ('ol ma sa) 1. a certain small berry. - 2. a small weight'.

1. vb. and adj. to be worthy, suitable; becoming, appropriate, with termin. inf., in later times and vulgo, with the root, sbyín-par _os (sbyin par 'os) it is becoming, it is meet to give; _di yzigs-par mi _os ('di gzigs par mi 'os) it is not decent to see this; *ka-lón čá-če 'os* (ka lon cha che 'os) W. he is worthy to be a vizier; *ť-sam la taṅ mi 'os* (th sam la tang mi 'os) W. he is not worth such high wages; *la nán-te taṅ 'os* (la nan te tang 'os) W. he deserves extra-pay; yid-smón _os (yid smon 'os) to be wished, desirable; pýag bya-bar _os-par gyur (phyag bya bar 'os par gyur) he becomes adorable; stód-_os (stod 'os) to be praised, laudable; bkúr-_os (bkur 'os) deserving honour Cs.; tams-čád-la póg-_os-pai čád-pa (thams cad la phog 'os pa'i chad pa) the punishment condign to all; rarely with genit.: kún-gyis bkúr-žiṅ mčód-pai _os (kun gyis bkur zhing mchod pa'i 'os) Mil. he is deserving of universal honour and respect, and even: rjei _os min (rje'i 'os min) he was not worth to be a king, for which more frq. the termin. is used: ytsó-mor _ós-pa žig (gtso mor 'os pa zhig) Glr. the one that is the most deserving of being mistress, i. e. she that has the genteelest appearance, that is most of a gentlewoman; grógs-su _ós-pa (grogs su 'os pa) he is worthy to be his colleague, nif. Mil. - 2. more particularly in colloquial language: right, W. *ós-čan* ('os can), *ŏ-šan* ('o shan); with a negative *mi-ós-pa* (mi 'os pa), *os-méd* ('os med), *os-mín* ('os min) W., *mi-ŏ-pa* (mi 'o pa) etc. C. wrong (for the rigs-pa (rigs pa) and mi-rigs-pa (mi rigs pa) of earlier lit.); mi _ós-pai spyod-pa byed-pa (mi 'os pa'i spyod pa byed pa) Glr. to entertain illicit intercourse; rdzas _ós-pa (rdzas 'os pa) a lawful, mi _ós-pa (mi 'os pa) an unlawful matter Schr.; *ŏ-min-ghi ṭim-gál* ('o min ghi khrim gal) C. a wrong, immoral act, sinful transgression; *'os mi-ós ṕé-če* ('os mi 'os phe che) W. to discern between right and wrong; with regard to a man's words, credible, trustworthy, or the contrary. - Sch. has besides: _os či yod ('os ci yod), 'what other means or way is there?' and: _os spyi-ba ('os spyi ba) 'to finish (a thing) for the most part; to be good or tolerably good' (??).
